figures.caption: Fig. 4: Qualitative result of depth estimation on TartanAir [31] and ScanNet [8] datasets. Our method refines geometry-consistent novel views within the extrapolated artifact areas, leveling up the 3DGS representation to achieve complete scene reconstruction with superior geometry detail recovery and boundary coherence.

Table 1: Quantitative comparison on MipNeRF360 [4] and VRNeRF [37] datasets with sparse two-views input. The best result is in bold, and the secondbest is underlined. All experiments are conducted with the same baseline [13].
Fig. 3: Qualitative result on MipNeRF360 [4] and VRNeRF [37] datasets.
Fig. 6: Qualitative result by removing geometry tokens and palette filtering.
Table 3: Quantitative evaluation of view-dependency on the ScanNet dataset. We compare our method against AnySplat across varying numbers of input views. Bold numbers indicate the best performance.

Fig. 2: Overview of the Leveling3D pipeline. Our pipeline integrates a geometryaware leveling adapter that fuses geometry tokens with diffusion control, enabling robust refinement of extrapolated views and leveling up the 3D reconstruction with extended geometric-consistent extrapolation areas.
